zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Cecil於1520年9月13日出生在英國林可爾史爾包恩時，他的父親Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l25歲，母親Jane Heckington19歲。他於1541年5月8日與Mary Cheke在英國倫敦結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1598年8月4日在英國米德萊可維斯特明斯特享年77歲，埋葬在英國林可爾史爾斯坦福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Cecil于1520年9月13日出生在英国林可爾史爾包恩时，他的父亲Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l25岁，母亲Jane Heckington19岁。他于1541年5月8日与Mary Cheke在英国倫敦结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1598年8月4日在英国米德萊可維斯特明斯特享年77岁，埋葬在英国林可爾史爾斯坦福。

nameMeanings.name: Cecil
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh (Monmouthshire): from the Old Welsh personal name Seisyllt, sometimes wrongly said to derive from the Latin name Caecilius (see Sisley ). The most frequent modern spelling is a Renaissance attempt to connect the surname with Caecilius in the English pronunciation of its time. The Welsh name is more probably, but not definitely, from the Latin name Sextilius, a derivative of sextus ‘sixth’.

History: The great and powerful English Cecil family first came to prominence with David Cecil, a Monmouthshire gentleman who espoused the cause of Henry Tudor and came to court in London after the latter became king in 1485. His grandson William Cecil, Lord Burghley (1520–98), was Elizabeth I's chief adviser for 40 years, and his descendants have remained politically powerful and culturally influential in Britain ever since. They were originally minor Welsh gentry; their name is found in a variety of forms, including Sitsylt, Ceyssel, and Sisseld.

variants.name: Cissell
meanings: English (Middlesex): variant of Welsh   Cecil  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Sisley
meanings: English (Kent): from the popular Middle English female personal name Sisely (Old French Cecile, Cicile, from Latin Caecilia, feminine form of the Roman surname Caecilius, a derivative of caecus ‘blind’). This was the name of a early Christian virgin martyr of the 2nd or 3rd century, who came to be regarded as the patron saint of music. The surname is generally spelt with C- in the Middle Ages, following the trend of the personal name, and with S- in modern times. Compare Cecil and see also Sise , Sisson .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Cecil was born on 13 September 1520, in Bourne, Lincolnshire, England, his father, Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l, was 25 and his mother, Jane Heckington, was 19. He married Mary Cheke on 8 May 1541, in London, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 4 August 1598, in Westminster, Middlesex, England, at the age of 77, and was buried in Stamford, Lincolnshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Cecil est né le 13 septembre 1520, à Bourne, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Jane Heckington, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Mary Cheke le 8 mai 1541, à Londres, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 4 août 1598, à Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 77 ans, et a été enterré à Stamford, Lincolnshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Cecil am 13. September 1520 in Bourne, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l 25 und seine Mutter Jane Heckington 19. Er hat am 8. Mai 1541 in London, England Mary Cheke geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 4. August 1598 im Alter von 77 Jahren in Westminster, Middlesex, England und wurde in Stamford, Lincolnshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Cecil è nato il il 13 settembre 1520 nel Bourne, Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Jane Heckington, aveva 19 anni. Lui sposato Mary Cheke il 8 maggio 1541, nel London, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 4 agosto 1598, nel Westminster, Middlesex, England, all'età di 77 anni, e fu sepolto a Stamford, Lincolnshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Cecilが1520年9月13日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアボウルンで生まれた時，父Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil lは25歳，母Jane Heckingtonは19歳であった。1541年5月8日にイギリスロンドンでMary Chekeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1598年8月4日にイギリスミッドレセックスウエストミンスターで77歳で死去し，イギリスリンコルンシュアイアスタムフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Cecil(이)가 1520년 9월 13일 영국 링코실르 본에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane Heckington(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1541년 5월 8일 영국 런던에서 Mary Cheke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1598년 8월 4일 영국 미들레세스 웨스트민스터에서 77세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 링코실르 스타퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.13 September 1520–4 August 1598: 13 de setembro de 1520–4 de agosto de 1598
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Cecil nasceu em 13 de setembro de 1520, em Bourne, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Jane Heckington, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Cheke em 8 de maio de 1541, em London, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 4 de agosto de 1598, em Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, com 77 anos, e foi sepultado em Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Cecil родился 13 сентября 1520 г. в местности бурн, линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane Heckington, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Cheke 8 мая 1541 г. в местности Лондон, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 4 августа 1598 г. в местности вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия в возрасте 77 лет и был похоронен в местности стамфорд, линколншир, Англия.

es.13 September 1520–4 August 1598: 13 de septiembre de 1520–4 de agosto de 1598
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William Cecil nació el 13 de septiembre de 1520, en Bourne, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Jane Heckington, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Mary Cheke el 8 de mayo de 1541, en London, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 4 de agosto de 1598, en Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 77 años, y fue sepultado en Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.
